content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves gently to the left ahead with the car positioned slightly right of center but well within track boundaries and on course.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 57, target = 57 + 5 = 62
- Waypoint 62: (3.7827706336975098, 3.7506459951400757)
- Car position: (4.458, 3.2609)
- Bearing = atan2(3.7506 - 3.2609, 3.7828 - 4.458) = atan2(0.4897, -0.6752)
- = 180 - atan2(0.4897, 0.6752) = 180 - 35.93° = 144.07°
- Current heading: 139.80°
- Delta = 144.07 - 139.80 = +4.27° → very slight left, nearly straight

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves gently left ahead, delta to waypoint 62 is +4.27° which is under 5°, so car is essentially on the line — using steering_angle=0 to avoid overshoot oscillation.",

    "Track has a left-curving section around waypoints 55-70, heading southwest",
    "Car is consistently slightly right of center (~0.21m) but within safe margin (track width 0.76m)",
    "Full circuit ~17.7m, speed 1.5 is max and appropriate for moderate curves",
    "Use steering_angle=0 for |delta| <5° to avoid oscillation on near-straight sections",
    "Delta near +6-9° means gentle left correction — use steering_angle=5",
    "Car is maintaining 1.5 speed well through this section",
    "Waypoints 60-70 show continuing left curve (south-southwest)",
    "Heading ~140°, track continues curving left toward waypoints 65-70",
    "5° steering is appropriate for delta 5-10° range to avoid overshoot",
    "When delta drops below 5°, immediately switch to 0 steering to prevent oscillation"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [4.6841, 4.6133, 4.534, 4.458],
    "y": [3.0645, 3.1285, 3.1977, 3.2609],
    "yaw": [140.753, 140.033, 139.698, 139.804]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": 4.27,
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 0.0
}
```
